Class AppCache

java.lang.Object
com.example.codebase.AppCache
public class AppCache extends Object
Simple singleton memory cache for profile and event data. Avoids fetching from Firestore every time a screen opens by storing the current user and event list in memory for the duration of the app session. Cache is cleared on logout or explicit clear() call.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clear()
    Clears all cached data.
    List<Event>
    getCachedEvents()
    Returns the currently cached list of events.
    User

    Returns the currently cached user.
    static AppCache
    getInstance()
    Returns the singleton instance of AppCache.
    boolean
    hasCachedEvents()
    Returns whether any events are currently stored in the cache.
    boolean
    hasCachedUser()
    Returns whether a user is currently stored in the cache.
    void

    Stores a list of events in the cache.
    void

    Stores a user in the cache.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static AppCache getInstance()
      Returns the singleton instance of AppCache. Creates a new instance if one does not already exist. Thread-safe via synchronization.
      Returns:
      the shared AppCache instance

    • getCachedUser

      public User getCachedUser()
      Returns the currently cached user.
      Returns:
      the cached User, or null if not set

    • setCachedUser

      public void setCachedUser(User cachedUser)
      Stores a user in the cache.
      Parameters:
      cachedUser - the User to cache

    • getCachedEvents

      public List<Event> getCachedEvents()
      Returns the currently cached list of events.
      Returns:
      list of cached Event objects, never null

    • setCachedEvents

      public void setCachedEvents(List<Event> cachedEvents)
      Stores a list of events in the cache. If null is passed, an empty list is stored instead.
      Parameters:
      cachedEvents - the list of Event objects to cache

    • hasCachedUser

      public boolean hasCachedUser()
      Returns whether a user is currently stored in the cache.
      Returns:
      true if a cached user exists, false otherwise

    • hasCachedEvents

      public boolean hasCachedEvents()
      Returns whether any events are currently stored in the cache.
      Returns:
      true if the cached event list is non-null and non-empty, false otherwise

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears all cached data. Sets the cached user to null and resets the event list to empty. Should be called on user logout or session end.
